subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: An insurance company insures a large number of homes. The insured value, $X$, of a randomly selected home is assumed to follow a distribution with density function $f(x) = \begin{cases} 3x^{-4} & x > 1 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ Given that a randomly selected home is insured for at least 1.5, what is the probability that it is insured for less than 2?
